us-politics.headline: US F-15 Downed Over Iran, Crew Missing
us-politics.body:
Iran shot down a US F-15 fighter jet, with two crew members unaccounted for and a search underway. Trump was briefed on the incident as the White House simultaneously released a record $1.5 trillion Pentagon budget request for 2027 - the largest defense spending ask in American history. The timing puts the administration under immediate pressure over its Middle East posture.

Senator Van Hollen pushed back on escalation fears, arguing Iran has no intercontinental ballistic missile capable of reaching the US. The Washington Post reports Trump is already seeking a political reset, with the conflict weighing on gas prices and midterm calculations. How the administration responds to the downed jet will define the next phase of US-Iran tensions.

sources.title: President Donald Trump briefed on downed F-15 fighter jet in Iran
U.S. fighter jet crashes in Iran; search launched for 2 crew members
Live updates: Iran shoots down US F-15; White House’s 2027 budget seeks $1.5T for Pentagon
Trump requests record-breaking budget of $1.5 trillion for Pentagon
Trump seeks $1.5 trillion for defense spending in 2027 budget request
Van Hollen: Iran ‘nowhere near’ any ICBM that can reach US
White House requests $1.5 trillion for defense spending in 2027 budget
Reeling from war in the Middle East, Trump reaches for a reset
US fighter jet downed, Iranian media reports

us-politics.headline: Cabinet Turmoil: Bondi Out, Hegseth Fires Army Chief
us-politics.body:
The Trump cabinet is fracturing. Attorney General Pam Bondi was fired, and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth dismissed the Army Chief of Staff - a move that drew an immediate rebuke from Republican Representative Rich McCormick, who said he would investigate. Fox News reports further departures are possible, with several officials now under pressure.

Democrats are drawing their own conclusions. Rep. Seth Moulton noted Bondi was removed just 12 days before a scheduled Epstein deposition. Rep. Swalwell denied Bondi had tipped him off about any report. Anthony Scaramucci, a Trump critic, argued Bondi simply wasn't delivering enough for the president. Trump is already eyeing a new attorney general, though at least one key Republican senator has signaled a potential confirmation fight.

sources.title: Trump cabinet shakeup expands after Noem exit, Bondi firing — who’s under pressure next?
GOP rep on Hegseth firing Army chief of staff: ‘I will look into it immediately’
Swalwell says Bondi didn’t tip him off on report
Trump eyes next attorney general as key GOP senator signals potential roadblock
Scaramucci: Bondi wasn’t doing enough for Trump
Moulton: Trump ‘got rid’ of Bondi 12 days before Epstein deposition

us-politics.headline: Vance Named Fraud Czar; ICE Records Fight Escalates
us-politics.body:
Trump announced Vice President Vance will serve as a so-called fraud czar, with a stated focus on blue states. Critics see it as a political targeting exercise. Supporters frame it as overdue accountability for Democratic-run jurisdictions.

Separately, a state attorney general moved to block what Fox News describes as a county-level effort to restrict DHS records access and limit ICE cooperation. The county denies deliberately freezing out federal immigration enforcement. The standoff reflects the ongoing friction between federal immigration priorities and local governments resisting cooperation - a conflict that shows no sign of resolution.

sources.title: State top cop moves to crush alleged DHS records restriction as county denies ICE-out
Trump: Vance will primarily focus on blue states as ‘fraud czar’

us-politics.headline: Opioid Funds at Risk; AI and Health Data Raise Alarms
us-politics.body:
Two domestic policy warnings surfaced Thursday. A Hill opinion piece cautioned that billions in opioid settlement money risk being diverted away from addiction treatment, echoing concerns from public health advocates who watched earlier settlement funds get absorbed into general state budgets. The stakes are high - the opioid crisis still kills tens of thousands of Americans annually.

On a separate front, health privacy experts warned that existing laws like HIPAA do not cover the data AI tools collect when patients use health-related chatbots or apps. As AI becomes embedded in healthcare, a significant regulatory gap leaves sensitive personal health information exposed with little legal recourse for consumers.

sources.title: Beware Dr. Chatbot: Privacy laws don’t protect health care data from AI
America must not waste an opioid settlement lifeline

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Bites as Australia's Reserves Run Critically Low
au-politics.body:
Australia's fuel supply is in serious trouble. Industry groups warn that incoming shipments will not cover even a month of national consumption, putting pressure on Energy Minister Chris Bowen to act fast. The government has faced criticism for being slow to address long-standing vulnerabilities in the country's fuel storage and import capacity.

Bowen has defended the government's response, pointing to ongoing negotiations with suppliers and emergency contingency measures. But industry insiders say the structural problem runs deeper than any short-term fix. Australia's reliance on imported refined fuel, with minimal domestic reserves, leaves it exposed to any disruption in global supply chains.

sources.title: Energy minister Chris Bowen on the impact of the fuel crisis - podcast
Fuel heading to Australia won't last a month, industry says

au-politics.headline: Tax Policy Divides Parties as Election Pressure Mounts
au-politics.body:
Both Labor and the Coalition are under growing pressure to sharpen their tax policies ahead of the election, with voters demanding action on cost-of-living and budget repair. Notably, Liberal MP Andrew Hastie has broken with orthodox Coalition thinking by suggesting miners could reasonably pay more tax - a signal that internal debate on the right is live.

Albanese faces his own challenge. Trump's military action against Iran has shifted the geopolitical backdrop sharply, and some analysts argue it should push Labor toward bolder economic and defence reforms rather than cautious incrementalism. Neither party has yet produced a tax platform that fully satisfies voters, and the gap between rhetoric and policy detail remains wide on both sides.

sources.title: Taxing times for Albanese and Taylor as parties seek to match voter expectations
Trump’s Iran war has woken Albanese up to a new reality. Will it spur him towards ‘ambitious’ reforms?

au-politics.headline: Queensland Man Jailed 15 Years for Live-Streamed Child Abuse
au-politics.body:
A Queensland man from North Lakes has been sentenced to 15 years in prison after live-streaming the sexual abuse of a child. The case highlights the growing use of online platforms to facilitate and broadcast child exploitation, a trend that has alarmed law enforcement agencies across Australia.

The sentence reflects courts taking an increasingly firm stance on offences involving digital distribution of abuse material. Authorities have repeatedly called for stronger international cooperation to shut down the platforms used to share such content.

sources.title: Queensland man jailed for 15 years after live-streaming child abuse

au-politics.headline: Myanmar Junta Chief Consolidates Power with Presidential Role
au-politics.body:
Myanmar's military chief Min Aung Hlaing has been elected president, cementing the junta's grip on power more than four years after the 2021 coup. The move formalises what has long been the reality on the ground - that Hlaing controls the state - but gives his rule a veneer of institutional legitimacy.

The development has drawn international condemnation. Australia, along with Western allies, has consistently refused to recognise the junta as Myanmar's legitimate government. The election is widely seen as a staged process designed to entrench military rule rather than signal any genuine democratic transition.

sources.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing elected president

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Pilots Downed, Strikes Disputed
world.body:
Iran shot down at least one US fighter jet and launched search operations for the crew, releasing footage of American aircraft combing the area. Iranian missiles also struck targets inside Israel, with drone footage showing factory damage from debris. The exchange marks a sharp escalation in direct military confrontation between the two countries.

Controversy is mounting over a US strike on a sports hall in the Iranian city of Lamerd, with international law experts disputing Washington's account of the target. A Red Crescent supply warehouse in southwest Iran was also attacked. Trump, meanwhile, suggested the US could eventually seize Iranian oil, raising the stakes further as analysts question when - and how - the conflict ends.

sources.title: Drone footage shows a factory in Israel damaged by Iranian missile debris
Iranian forces launch search for crew of downed US fighter jet
Trump says with more time, US can ‘take the oil’ in Iran
Red Crescent supply warehouse attacked in southwest Iran
Iran release videos of US aircraft searching for pilots shot down in Iran
Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
How the US and Israel are waging war on Iran’s medicines, vaccines
International law experts allege violations in Iran war
When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines

world.headline: Trump Requests Record $1.5 Trillion Defence Budget
world.body:
The Trump administration has asked Congress to approve a historic $1.5 trillion defence budget, the largest military spending request in US history. The proposal pairs the surge in military funding with deep cuts to domestic programmes, setting up a fierce battle on Capitol Hill.

The request comes as the US wages active war against Iran and Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth pushed out the Army's top general, with Christopher LaNeve set to take command. Trump also fired Attorney General Pam Bondi. The moves signal a sweeping reshaping of both military leadership and the broader federal government.

sources.title: Trump seeks historic $1.5 trillion for military in Congress budget request
Trump seeks massive $1.5tn for defence alongside cuts in domestic spending
Pete Hegseth asks US Army's top general to step down
Who is Christopher LaNeve, set to lead the US Army?
Trump removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi

world.headline: Ukraine Gains Ground as Russia's Resources Thin
world.body:
Ukraine has slowed Russian advances along key front lines, recaptured territory, and is steadily depleting Moscow's war chest, according to new reporting. The gains are modest but mark a shift from the grinding losses of earlier months. Kyiv is framing the progress as evidence that sustained Western support is working.

Russia continues to press attacks but at mounting cost. Ukrainian officials say attrition is increasingly working in their favour, though the front remains volatile and a decisive breakthrough remains elusive.

sources.title: Ukraine slows enemy advances, liberates land, drains Russia’s war chest

world.headline: Greece Scandal, Cuba Releases, France Ban Overturned
world.body:
Greek ministers resigned over an EU farming subsidy scandal, deepening a political crisis in Athens just as Brussels scrutinises member states' use of agricultural funds. Cuba announced it will release more than 2,000 prisoners under growing US pressure, a significant concession from Havana. In France, courts overturned a government ban on a Muslim gathering, ruling the prohibition unlawful.

Italy fired national football coach Gennaro Gattuso after the team failed to qualify for the 2026 World Cup - a humiliation for one of the sport's historic powers. The Uffizi gallery in Florence confirmed a cyber-attack but insisted no collections data was compromised.

sources.title: Judge dismisses most of Blake Lively's claims in Justin Baldoni harassment suit
Italy's famed Uffizi admits cyber-attack but denies security breach
Cuba to release more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
Greek ministers resign over EU farming subsidy scandal
Gattuso out as Italy’s coach after failure to qualify for 2026 World Cup
Italy part company with manager after failing to qualify for World Cup
France's Muslim gathering ban overturned by courts

ai.headline: OpenAI Buys Talk Show, Raising Conflict-of-Interest Alarms
ai.body:
OpenAI has acquired TBPN, a founder-focused tech talk show with a loyal Silicon Valley audience, in what critics are calling a straightforward purchase of favorable media coverage. The deal is OpenAI's latest distraction from its core business - it follows a string of acquisitions and side projects that have little to do with building AI.

Wired frames the move bluntly: OpenAI bought itself good press. TBPN's hosts have been reliably bullish on the company, and nothing in the acquisition structure changes that dynamic. The purchase raises hard questions about editorial independence at a moment when AI companies are under growing public scrutiny.

sources.title: OpenAI takes on another "side quest," buys tech-focused talk show TBPN
OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show ‘TBPN’—and Buys Itself Some Positive News
OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show

ai.headline: AI Chatbots Now Prescribing Psychiatric Drugs. Seriously.
ai.body:
AI chatbots are moving into psychiatric care, prescribing and refilling medications for conditions like anxiety and depression with minimal human oversight. The Verge reports that some platforms are operating in regulatory grey zones, exploiting gaps in telehealth rules that were loosened during the pandemic and never fully tightened.

The stakes are high. Psychiatric medications carry serious risks, require careful dosing, and demand ongoing clinical judgment. Handing that process to a chatbot - even a capable one - removes the human accountability that catches errors. Regulators have not caught up. That gap is now a product.

sources.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs

ai.headline: Perplexity's Privacy Claims Collapse Under Legal Challenge
ai.body:
A lawsuit filed against Perplexity AI alleges its much-promoted "Incognito Mode" does not actually protect user privacy, calling it a sham. The complaint suggests the feature offers cosmetic cover while the company continues collecting data in ways users would not expect or consent to if fully informed.

The case lands at a bad time for AI search companies trying to win user trust. Perplexity has positioned itself as a cleaner, more transparent alternative to Google. If the lawsuit's claims hold up, that positioning takes serious damage. A separate warning about Granola, an AI note-taking app, surfaced this week too - its notes are publicly accessible by default to anyone with a link.

sources.title: Perplexity's "Incognito Mode" is a "sham," lawsuit says
PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default

ai.headline: Rowhammer Returns, Now Targeting Nvidia GPU Machines
ai.body:
Researchers have demonstrated new Rowhammer attacks capable of giving attackers complete control over machines running Nvidia GPUs. The technique exploits physical memory vulnerabilities that have proven stubbornly difficult to patch, and the GPU angle is significant - these are the machines powering AI workloads, cloud inference, and high-performance computing at scale.

Rowhammer attacks work by repeatedly accessing memory rows to flip bits in adjacent rows, corrupting data or escalating privileges without touching protected code. The fact that Nvidia GPU systems are now viable targets expands the threat surface considerably. Cloud providers and AI infrastructure operators will need to assess their exposure fast.

sources.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs

btc.headline: Bitcoin Slides Below $70K Into a Thin Holiday Weekend
btc.body:
Bitcoin dipped below $70,000 heading into Good Friday, with ETF inflows and CME futures trading set to go dark for the long weekend. Futures markets have tilted bearish, volatility has cooled, and the proportion of Bitcoin supply held in profit has dropped to levels analysts associate with true bear markets. The timing is awkward. Without institutional flow from ETFs or CME to provide a floor, BTC is exposed to thin liquidity conditions over the holiday.

Cathie Wood offered a contrarian read, arguing Bitcoin is done with its historic 85% drawdown cycles and setting a new floor target near $34,000. Meanwhile, corporate treasury strategies are fracturing, with some firms diverging from the MicroStrategy playbook as prices soften.

sources.title: Crypto Biz: Bitcoin treasuries break ranks as BTC dips below $70K
Price predictions 4/3: BTC, ETH, BNB, XRP, SOL, DOGE, HYPE, ADA, BCH, LINK
Bitcoin 'done' with 85% crashes, says Cathie Wood amid new $34K target
Crypto snoozes into Good Friday as oil and macro stir: Crypto Daybook Americas
Crypto consolidates as volatility cools and futures markets tilt bearish
Bitcoin supply in profit heads to ‘true bear market’ levels
Bitcoin heads into holiday weekend exposed as ETF and CME flows go offline

btc.headline: DeFi Loses $169M to Hacks as Execution Risk Rises
btc.body:
Crypto hackers drained $169 million from 34 DeFi protocols in Q1 2026, according to DefiLlama data. The figure marks a decline from prior quarters, but the threat profile is shifting. Analysts now argue execution risk - the danger of losing funds through bad trades, liquidations, or protocol mechanics - has overtaken custody risk as the primary concern for DeFi users.

A Bank of Canada study on Aave v3 illustrates the point. The protocol avoided bad debt during volatile periods by pushing losses onto borrowers through aggressive liquidation mechanics, effectively transferring systemic risk rather than eliminating it. Separately, Drift Protocol sent an onchain message to wallets linked to a $280 million exploit, a public attempt to open negotiations with the attacker.

sources.title: Execution risk in crypto is the new custody risk
Aave avoided bad debt by shifting risk to borrowers: Bank of Canada study
Drift sends onchain message to wallets tied to $280M exploit
Crypto hackers steal $169M from 34 DeFi protocols in Q1: DefiLlama

btc.headline: Ethereum Foundation Hits Staking Target as L2 Scaling Debate Heats Up
btc.body:
The Ethereum Foundation completed a staking push, depositing another $93 million in ETH to reach its 70,000 ETH target. The move signals a more active treasury posture from the Foundation, which has faced criticism for sitting on large idle reserves while the network competes for developer and user attention.

On the infrastructure side, Offchain Labs argued that Ethereum Layer 2 networks need dynamic, responsive fee pricing to scale to billions of users. Fixed or slow-adjusting fee models create bottlenecks under load. The debate matters as L2s increasingly compete on cost and throughput, and as altcoins broadly outperformed a flat Bitcoin in Thursday trading.

sources.title: Ethereum L2s need responsive pricing to scale, says Offchain Labs
CoinDesk 20 performance update: Bitcoin (BTC) trades flat while altcoins rise
Ethereum Foundation stakes another $93 million ether, reaching its 70,000 ETH target

btc.headline: Quantum Threat Spurs New Chain as Regulatory Fault Lines Deepen
btc.body:
Naoris Protocol launched a quantum-resistant blockchain, positioning itself against what researchers call Q-Day - the point at which quantum computers could break the cryptographic foundations of Bitcoin and Ethereum. The launch reflects growing institutional anxiety about long-term network security, even if Q-Day remains years away.

On the regulatory front, Malta's clash with the European Securities and Markets Authority is shaping up as a proxy war over who controls crypto oversight in Europe. ESMA's push for centralized supervision threatens the regulatory autonomy smaller member states have used to attract crypto firms. The IMF added its own note of caution, publishing a report acknowledging that asset tokenization improves financial efficiency but introduces new systemic risks that regulators are not yet equipped to handle.

sources.title: Centralizing crypto: Why Malta’s clash with ESMA is about more than one small state
Naoris Protocol's quantum-resistant blockchain goes live as Bitcoin and Ethereum face 'Q-Day' threats
Tokenization makes finance more efficient but introduces risks: IMF

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and burnished
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust you'll get under the grill

exercise.name: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy dumbbells, open floor space
exercise.description: Cycle through farmer's carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries at varying distances, resting only between sets — brutally effective for grip, core stability, and real-world strength

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — philosophers have extended this to human cells, consciousness, and what makes you 'you' across time
learn.why: On a Friday it's worth sitting with the unsettling idea that the person who started this week and the person ending it share almost no identical atoms — Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' will rewire how you think about continuity, memory, and selfhood
